preamble: The voiceless postalveolar fricative [ʃ] shifts to the voiceless alveolar affricate [ts], representing a complex change involving both place retraction and affrication. This process is attested in historical sound change and phonological variation across multiple language families.

examples.note: Historical shift from postalveolar to alveolar with affrication in Proto-Slavic evolution.
Phonetic realization where [ʃ] is adapted to native [ts] patterns in loanword adaptation.
Allophonic variation in certain phonological contexts or in rapid speech.
